en: And he began to speak to them in parables. "A man planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a pit for the winepress and built a tower, and leased it to tenants and went into another country.
zh: 耶穌又用比喻對他們說：“有一個人栽種了一個葡萄園，四面圍上籬笆，挖了一個壓酒池，蓋了一座瞭望臺，然後租給佃戶，就遠行去了。

en: When the season came, he sent a servant to the tenants to get from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
zh: 到了時候，園主派了一個僕人到佃戶那裡，向佃戶收取葡萄園一部分的果子。

en: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
zh: 佃戶抓住他，打了他，放他空手回去。

en: Again he sent to them another servant, and they struck him on the head and treated him shamefully.
zh: 園主再派另外一個僕人到他們那裡，他們打傷了他的頭，並且侮辱他。

en: And he sent another, and him they killed. And so with many others: some they beat, and some they killed.
zh: 園主又派另一個去，他們就把他殺了。後來又派去許多僕人，有的給他們打了，有的給他們殺了。

en: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
zh: 還有一個，就是園主的愛子，最後園主派他到那裡去，說：‘他們必尊敬我的兒子。’

en: But those tenants said to one another, 'This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'
zh: 那些佃戶卻彼此說：‘這是繼承產業的；來，我們殺了他，產業就是我們的了。’

en: And they took him and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.
zh: 於是抓住他，把他殺了，扔在葡萄園外。

en: What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
zh: 這樣，葡萄園的主人要怎麼辦呢？他要來除掉那些佃戶，把葡萄園租給別人。

en: Have you not read this Scripture: "' The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
zh: 你們沒有念過這段聖經嗎： ‘建築工人所棄的石頭， 成了房角的主要石頭；

en: this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes'?"
zh: 這是主所作的， 在我們眼中看為希奇。’”

en: And they were seeking to arrest him but feared the people, for they perceived that he had told the parable against them. So they left him and went away.
zh: 他們知道他這比喻是針對他們說的，就想要捉拿他，但因為害怕群眾，只好離開他走了。

en: And they sent to him some of the Pharisees and some of the Herodians, to trap him in his talk.
zh: 後來，他們派了幾個法利賽人和希律黨的人到耶穌那裡去，要找他的把柄來陷害他。

en: And they came and said to him, "Teacher, we know that you are true and do not care about anyone's opinion. For you are not swayed by appearances, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Should we pay them, or should we not?"
zh: 他們來到了，就對他說：“老師，我們知道你為人誠實，不顧忌任何人，因為你不徇情面，只照著真理把 神的道教導人。請問納稅給凱撒可以不可以？我們該納不該納呢？”

en: But, knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, "Why put me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me look at it."
zh: 耶穌看出他們的假意，就對他們說：“你們為甚麼試探我呢？拿一個銀幣來給我，讓我看看。”

en: And they brought one. And he said to them, "Whose likeness and inscription is this?" They said to him, "Caesar's."
zh: 他們就拿來了。耶穌問他們：“這是誰的像，誰的名號？”他們回答他：“凱撒的。”

en: Jesus said to them, "Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's." And they marveled at him.
zh: 耶穌說：“凱撒的應當歸給凱撒， 神的應當歸給 神。”他們就對他十分驚奇。

en: And Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection. And they asked him a question, saying,
zh: 撒都該人向來認為沒有復活的事，他們來到耶穌那裡，問他：

en: "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife, but leaves no child, the man must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
zh: “老師，摩西曾寫給我們說：‘如果一個人死了，留下妻子，沒有兒女，他的弟弟就應當娶他的妻子，為哥哥立後。’

en: There were seven brothers; the first took a wife, and when he died left no offspring.
zh: 從前有兄弟七人，頭一個娶了妻子，死了，沒有留下孩子；

en: And the second took her, and died, leaving no offspring. And the third likewise.
zh: 第二個娶了她，也沒有留下孩子，就死了；第三個也是這樣。

en: And the seven left no offspring. Last of all the woman also died.
zh: 那七個人都沒有留下孩子，最後那女人也死了。

en: In the resurrection, when they rise again, whose wife will she be? For the seven had her as wife."
zh: 到了復活的時候，他們都要復活，她是哪一個的妻子呢？因為七個人都娶過她。”

en: Jesus said to them, "Is this not the reason you are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God?
zh: 耶穌對他們說：“你們錯了，不正是因為你們不明白聖經，也不曉得 神的能力嗎？

en: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
zh: 因為人從死裡復活以後，也不娶，也不嫁，而是像天上的天使一樣。

en: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God spoke to him, saying, 'I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?
zh: 關於死人復活的事，摩西的經卷中荊棘篇上， 神怎樣對他說：‘我是亞伯拉罕的 神，以撒的 神，雅各的 神’，你們沒有念過嗎？

en: He is not God of the dead, but of the living. You are quite wrong."
zh: 他不是死人的 神，而是活人的 神。你們是大錯特錯了！”

en: And one of the scribes came up and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, asked him, "Which commandment is the most important of all?"
zh: 有一個經學家，聽到他們的辯論，覺得耶穌回答得好，就來問他：“誡命中哪一條是第一重要的呢？”

en: Jesus answered, "The most important is, 'Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
zh: 耶穌回答：“第一重要的是：‘以色列啊，你要聽！主我們的 神是獨一的主。

en: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.'
zh: 你要全心、全性、全意、全力，愛主你的 神。’

en: The second is this: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these."
zh: 其次是：‘要愛人如己。’再沒有別的誡命比這兩條更重要的了。”

en: And the scribe said to him, "You are right, Teacher. You have truly said that he is one, and there is no other besides him.
zh: 那經學家對耶穌說：“老師，是的，你說的很對， 神是獨一的，除了他以外再沒有別的 神。

en: And to love him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength, and to love one's neighbor as oneself, is much more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices."
zh: 我們要用全心、全意、全力去愛他，並且要愛人如己，這就比一切燔祭和各樣祭物好得多了。”

en: And when Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And after that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
zh: 耶穌見他回答得有智慧，就對他說：“你距離 神的國不遠了。”從此再也沒有人敢問他了。

en: And as Jesus taught in the temple, he said, "How can the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
zh: 耶穌在殿裡教訓人，說：“經學家怎麼說基督是大衛的子孫呢？

en: David himself, in the Holy Spirit, declared, "' The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.'
zh: 大衛自己被聖靈感動卻說： ‘主對我的主說： 你坐在我的右邊， 等我把你的仇敵放在你的腳下 。’

en: David himself calls him Lord. So how is he his son?" And the great throng heard him gladly.
zh: 大衛自己既然稱他為主，他又怎麼會是大衛的子孫呢？”群眾都喜歡聽他。

en: And in his teaching he said, "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes and like greetings in the marketplaces
zh: 耶穌教導人的時候，說：“你們要提防經學家，他們喜歡穿長袍走來走去，喜歡人在市中心向他們問安，

en: and have the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
zh: 又喜歡會堂裡的高位，筵席上的首座。

en: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation."
zh: 他們吞沒了寡婦的房產，又假裝作冗長的禱告。這些人必受更重的刑罰。”

en: And he sat down opposite the treasury and watched the people putting money into the offering box. Many rich people put in large sums.
zh: 耶穌面對銀庫坐著，看著大家怎樣把錢投入庫中。許多有錢的人投入很多的錢。

en: And a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which make a penny.
zh: 後來，有一個窮寡婦來投入了兩個小錢，就是一個銅錢。

en: And he called his disciples to him and said to them, "Truly, I say to you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the offering box.
zh: 耶穌把門徒叫過來，對他們說：“我實在告訴你們，這窮寡婦投入庫裡的，比眾人投的更多。

en: For they all contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on."
zh: 因為他們都是把自己剩餘的投入，這寡婦是自己不足，卻把她一切所有的，就是全部養生的，都投進去了。”
